properties.name: Escondida Copper Mine
properties.significance: World's largest copper mine; ~5% of global copper supply
properties.price_impact: Strike (2017) removed ~1% global supply; copper +8% in days; BHP/union disputes are market events
properties.risk_factors: Water scarcity in Atacama; labor strikes (major 2017, 2022 near-strike); Chilean royalty/nationalization political risk; aging ore grade

properties.name: Los Pelambres Copper Mine
properties.significance: Major Chilean copper producer; Antofagasta is bellwether Chilean copper stock
properties.price_impact: Antofagasta production reports are copper market indicators
properties.risk_factors: Drought water supply constraints; community conflict; Chilean mining tax reform

properties.name: Grasberg Copper-Gold Mine
properties.significance: World's largest gold mine and one of largest copper mines
properties.price_impact: Disruptions move copper and gold markets simultaneously; smelting concentrate export bans (2023) created supply chain uncertainty
properties.risk_factors: Indonesian export concentrate ban (2023–24); separatist conflict in Papua; transition from open-pit to underground adds execution risk; government ownership leverage

properties.name: Morenci Copper Mine
properties.significance: Largest copper mine in North America; bellwether for US copper production
properties.price_impact: Freeport Q earnings with production guidance are major copper market events
properties.risk_factors: Water scarcity in Arizona; energy cost sensitivity; labor relations

properties.name: Muruntau Gold Mine
properties.significance: World's largest single open-pit gold mine by volume
properties.price_impact: State-owned; less production volatility; Uzbek gold exports are state revenue
properties.risk_factors: State control limits transparency; water scarcity; geopolitical position between Russia and China

properties.name: Olimpiada Gold Mine
properties.significance: Russia's largest gold mine; Polyus is world's 4th largest gold miner
properties.price_impact: Western sanctions on Polyus limited gold market access; Russian gold now flows to UAE, Turkey, China — creates market bifurcation
properties.risk_factors: Full US/EU sanctions on Polyus post-2022; gold sold via alternative channels; reputational risk for buyers

properties.name: Carlin Gold Trend
properties.significance: World's second largest gold district; largest gold producing region outside of South Africa/Australia
properties.price_impact: Barrick/Newmont production guidance moves gold market; combined JV major market factor
properties.risk_factors: Mature district with declining grades; water use conflicts with ranchers

properties.name: Kalgoorlie Super Pit
properties.significance: Australia's largest open-pit gold mine; iconic Australian mining asset
properties.price_impact: Part of Australia's ~300 tonnes/year gold output; Australian gold stocks track gold price closely
properties.risk_factors: Open-pit nearing end of economic life; underground transition underway

properties.name: Atacama Lithium Brine (Salar)
properties.operator: SQM (50%) / Albemarle (50%) — plus new Chilean state (CODELCO) JV
properties.significance: ~35% of global lithium supply; lowest cost lithium on earth
properties.price_impact: SQM production data is global lithium benchmark; Atacama output constrains EV battery supply chains
properties.risk_factors: Chilean nationalization risk (CODELCO state JV now required); water brine depletion; indigenous community rights (Atacameño); Chile's 'lithium strategy' adds regulatory uncertainty

properties.name: Greenbushes Lithium Mine
properties.capacity: ~1.6M tonnes lithium concentrate spodumene/year (world's largest hard rock deposit)
properties.significance: World's highest grade and largest hard-rock lithium mine
properties.price_impact: Greenbushes production sets spodumene price floor/ceiling; Tianqi's Chinese ownership creates geopolitical sensitivity
properties.risk_factors: Chinese ownership via Tianqi scrutinized by Australia FIRB; spodumene price volatility

properties.name: Bayan Obo Rare Earth Mine
properties.significance: World's largest rare earth deposit; China controls ~60% of global rare earth mining
properties.price_impact: China export restrictions (2023) on gallium/germanium were a supply shock precedent; REE embargo risk is existential for defense/EV supply chains
properties.risk_factors: Chinese state control; export quota/ban weapon; environmental processing issues; US/EU diversification efforts underway but years from scale

properties.name: Mountain Pass Rare Earth Mine
properties.significance: Only operating rare earth mine in USA; critical to US strategic supply chain
properties.price_impact: MP Materials key beneficiary of US REE policy; still sends concentrate to China for processing (bottleneck)
properties.risk_factors: Still dependent on China for separation/processing (building US separation facility); competition with Chinese pricing

properties.name: Ukraine Black Earth (Chernozem) Grain Belt
properties.capacity: ~80M tonnes grain/year potential; 60–70M tonnes actual pre-war
properties.significance: ~10% of global wheat, ~15% corn, ~50% sunflower oil; 'breadbasket of Europe'
properties.price_impact: Feb 2022 invasion spiked CBOT wheat +60% in weeks; Black Sea Grain Initiative lapse (July 2023) spiked wheat again; every harvest/export news moves ag markets
properties.risk_factors: Active war destroying farmland; export port closures (Odessa attacks); mine contamination; Russian attacks on grain infrastructure

properties.name: US Corn Belt
properties.operator: Private farms; ADM, Cargill, Bunge, Louis Dreyfus dominate trade
properties.significance: World's dominant corn and soybean producer/exporter; sets Chicago CBOT benchmark prices
properties.price_impact: USDA WASDE reports and crop condition weekly reports are the primary global grain price drivers; drought = major price spike
properties.risk_factors: La Niña/El Niño drought cycles; flooding (Mississippi River logistics); input cost inflation (fertilizer, energy)

properties.name: Brazil Cerrado (Soybean/Corn Belt)
properties.operator: Private farms; ADM, Cargill, Bunge, Louis Dreyfus, COFCO
properties.significance: World's largest soybean exporter; overtook USA as #1 in 2013; critical for China
properties.price_impact: Brazil production forecasts (CONAB) move CBOT soybeans; China-Brazil soy trade is largest ag commodity flow globally
properties.risk_factors: Deforestation/regulatory pressure; logistics bottlenecks (Paranaguá congestion); La Niña drought in south; Mato Grosso infrastructure dependency

properties.name: Argentina Pampas (Soy/Wheat)
properties.operator: Private farms; YPF Agro, Cresud; export via AGD, Aceitera General Deheza
properties.capacity: ~50M tonnes soybeans; ~20M tonnes wheat; ~50M tonnes corn
properties.significance: World's largest soy meal/oil exporter; major wheat and corn exporter
properties.price_impact: Argentina FX controls and export taxes frequently distort flows; 2023 drought cut soy -30% and spiked crush margins globally
properties.risk_factors: FX crisis and export tax policy (peso devaluation forces farmers to hold grain); El Niño/La Niña volatility; political instability

properties.name: Russian Wheat Belt (Black Soil Region)
properties.significance: Russia accounts for ~20% of global wheat exports; critical for Middle East/North Africa food security
properties.price_impact: Novorossiysk port closure risk; sanctions uncertainty on shipping/payments; Russia's market dominance in MENA makes it a price setter
properties.risk_factors: Black Sea export route risk; Western payment/insurance sanctions creating transaction complexity; food weapon risk for dependent nations

properties.name: Punjab / Haryana Grain Belt (India)
properties.significance: India periodic export bans (2022–23 wheat/rice) dislocated global markets; policy risk is high
properties.price_impact: India wheat export ban (May 2022) added to global food crisis; rice export ban (Aug 2023) spiked Thai rice prices +40%
properties.risk_factors: Export ban risk based on domestic inflation; monsoon dependency; groundwater depletion in Punjab

properties.name: Belaruskali Potash Mines
properties.significance: ~20% of global potash supply; Belarus + Russia = ~40% of global potash
properties.price_impact: 2021 Lukashenko sanctions cut Belarus potash exports; potash price tripled from $250 to $800/tonne (2021–22); has since normalized via Russia routing
properties.risk_factors: EU/US sanctions since 2021; traditional Baltic transit routes cut; alternative via Russian ports; sanction evasion via China/India

properties.name: Russian Potash & Fertilizer Hubs (Ural/Volga)
properties.capacity: ~15M tonnes potash; ~18M tonnes nitrogen fertilizer; ~10% global urea
properties.significance: Russia = world's largest fertilizer exporter by value; critical for global food production
properties.price_impact: Russia fertilizer export halt (2022) was major input cost shock globally; food inflation partly attributable to fertilizer squeeze; sanctions carve-outs now allow some flows
properties.risk_factors: Payment/shipping sanctions; EU/US sanctions with agricultural carve-outs; Russia used fertilizers as geopolitical leverage; India/China now primary buyers

properties.name: Port of Santos
properties.significance: Brazil's primary commodity export gateway; soybeans, sugar, orange juice, corn, coffee
properties.price_impact: Santos congestion during peak harvest season (Feb–April) affects CBOT soy basis; Paranaguá is overflow port
properties.risk_factors: Seasonal congestion; road/truck dependency from Mato Grosso (BR-163 bottleneck)

properties.name: Odessa / Black Sea Ukrainian Ports
properties.significance: Ukraine's grain export gateway; Black Sea Grain Initiative was centered here
properties.price_impact: Port closures trigger immediate global food price spikes; Feb 2022 invasion closed ports and sent wheat to all-time highs
properties.risk_factors: Active drone/missile attacks; Russian naval blockade capability; mined waters; Black Sea Grain Initiative collapsed July 2023

properties.name: Novorossiysk Port
properties.significance: Primary Russian grain AND oil export terminal; CPC oil pipeline terminus
properties.price_impact: Any disruption (drone attack, sanctions escalation) simultaneously affects oil AND grain markets — dual commodity shock risk
properties.risk_factors: Ukrainian drone attacks (2023); Western shipping insurance restrictions; CPC oil export point; Ukraine drone/naval drone attacks ongoing

properties.name: Shanghai / Yangshan Port
properties.significance: Global manufacturing hub export gateway; iron ore and copper imports; any closure is a global supply chain event
properties.price_impact: Shanghai COVID lockdown (2022) caused global shipping rates spike and supply chain disruption; Taiwan Strait escalation would immediately impact
properties.risk_factors: Taiwan Strait conflict risk; US-China trade war tariffs; COVID-type lockdown precedent; typhoon season

properties.name: North Atlantic Fishing Grounds
properties.significance: Key fishmeal and fish oil source; critical for global aquaculture feed and animal feed
properties.price_impact: Fishmeal price affects global chicken/pork/salmon prices; Peruvian anchoveta (Pacific) is larger but Atlantic complements
properties.risk_factors: Overfishing; climate-driven stock migration; El Niño impacts on Atlantic herring

properties.name: Peru Anchovy Fishing Zone (Humboldt Current)
properties.significance: ~50% of global fishmeal supply; critical input for aquaculture (salmon, shrimp), pig and poultry feed
properties.price_impact: El Niño = anchovy collapse = fishmeal +50–100%; impacts chicken/pork prices globally 6–12 months later
properties.risk_factors: El Niño is the single largest risk — 2023 El Niño cut catch severely; IMARPE quota decisions are market events

properties.name: Lithium Triangle – Argentina (Jujuy/Salta)
properties.operator: Lithium Americas, Allkem (Rio Tinto), POSCO, Ganfeng
properties.capacity: ~40k tonnes LCE/year current; major expansion pipeline
properties.significance: Argentina holds ~21% of global lithium reserves; part of the Lithium Triangle
properties.price_impact: Argentina lithium nationalism risk (nationalization proposals); Milei deregulation reversed some concerns; key to EV battery supply diversification
properties.risk_factors: Policy reversal risk; FX controls complicate revenue repatriation; Chinese investment dominance in processing partnerships

properties.name: DRC Copper-Cobalt Belt (Katanga)
properties.operator: Glencore, CMOC (Chinese), Ivanhoe Mines, various artisanal
properties.capacity: ~2M tonnes copper/year; ~70% of global cobalt supply
properties.significance: DRC = world's cobalt supply chokepoint; critical for EV batteries (LFP shift reduces but doesn't eliminate dependency)
properties.price_impact: Any DRC disruption is existential for cobalt markets; Glencore Katanga shutdowns (2019) spiked cobalt to $95k/tonne; Chinese CMOC dominance is strategic concern
properties.risk_factors: Civil conflict; Glencore FCPA/bribery cases; artisanal mining child labor; Chinese processing dependency; government royalty demands
